title: triticum turgidum subsp. asiaticum
intro: Triticum turgidum subsp. asiaticum, also known as bread wheat, is a subspecies of wheat that is native to Asia and is widely cultivated for its edible grains. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming bread wheat has been associated with various health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of certain cancers, and enhanced cognitive function. In herbal preparations, bread wheat is often used as a demulcent to soothe digestive issues and as an emollient to calm skin irritations, and it can be consumed as whole grains or in the form of flour, tea, or supplements.
